CRR.title: Current Run Rate
CRR.explanation: Think of this as the 'speed limit' the team is currently batting at. It's the average number of runs they score in every 6 balls (one over).
CRR.beginnerTip: A CRR of 10+ is usually very aggressive!

RRR.title: Required Run Rate
RRR.explanation: The 'target speed'. This is how fast the batting team needs to score to win the match before they run out of balls.
RRR.beginnerTip: If this number keeps going up, the batting team is in trouble!

Win Prob.title: Win Probability
Win Prob.explanation: A statistical guess of who will win, based on thousands of previous matches in similar situations.
Win Prob.beginnerTip: Cricket is unpredictable! A 90% chance doesn't mean it's over yet.

Momentum.title: Match Momentum
Momentum.explanation: This shows who is 'dominating' the game right now. High momentum means the team is hitting boundaries or taking wickets frequently.
Momentum.beginnerTip: Look for the spikes in the graph to see the turning points.
